title: oVirt / RHV Engine ERROR and SEVERE Log Burst Detection
app: Universal Forwarder with `monitor://` on `/var/log/ovirt-engine/engine.log`, optional Red Hat Insights (Splunkbase app 6439) for host-level correlation
spl:
index=virtualization sourcetype="ovirt:engine" earliest=-1h
| search log_level IN ("ERROR", "SEVERE", "FATAL")
| stats count as err_events by host, message
| where err_events>=5
| sort -err_events
description: The oVirt Engine is the control plane for RHV. Bursts of ERROR/SEVERE lines usually precede UI outages, stuck jobs, or database connectivity loss. Surfacing the dominant message text speeds triage compared to raw log tailing.
value: Shortens MTTR for virtualization control-plane incidents by quantifying which engine fault signature is dominating the cluster.
implementation: Use a dedicated LINE_BREAKER for multiline Java stack traces or ingest via a syslog relay that preserves severity. Extract `log_level` with transforms. Threshold alerts on rate per hour and on specific signatures (DB, Kerberos, certificate expiry).
visualization: Timechart (error rate), Table (top messages), Drilldown by correlation_id.

references.title: Red Hat Virtualization 4 — Logging and Reporting

knownFalsePositives: Cluster events during planned hardware refresh, capacity adds, or scheduled maintenance modes.
